name: Cheyenne (United States)

colors.symbolism: the surrounding border, the bucking-horse silhouette and the cattle brand, evoking Wyoming's leather-and-cattle heritage

description: A white field framed by a brown border, charged at the centre with a stylised brown bucking horse and rider set within a circular cattle-brand ring.
history: Cheyenne, the capital of Wyoming and a historic terminus of the Union Pacific Railroad and centre of the western cattle trade, uses the state's iconic Bucking Horse and Rider as the central device of its municipal flag.

features.role: bucking horse and rider — Wyoming's state symbol
features.symbolism: The 'Bucking Horse and Rider' is Wyoming's defining state emblem, evoking the state's ranching and rodeo heritage. Cheyenne, the state capital, hosts the long-running 'Frontier Days' rodeo and uses the bronco prominently on its civic flag.

features.role: stylised cattle-brand ring around the bucking-horse emblem
features.symbolism: Recalls the cattle brands of the open range and Cheyenne's role as a historic centre of the cattle trade.
